reviews.review_description:
Like Helldivers with space battles. Friendly community . Awesome game play loop. 

Who care if graphics are mid.

Also offline mode to ensure the game well always work.
If you like Star Trek and Star Wars Absolute 10/10 ..MUST BUY !!!

I own this game since a month now, and i can say two thing, number one is great job, this game is perfect, a good space game with a LOT of options, you can personalise your character, buy different ship, go on so many different type of missions, in space or on planets, guns and gears is upgradable, the spca map is huge and the daily quest is a good idea, plus the game is playable online too, BUT, the number two thing is, i played 90% of the time alone even if the game is made for groups because almost no one play, when you lunch the game you can go alone or in a public lobby, met 5 people i played with and 3 of them where mean, like complete idiots, crash up my mission for no reason, destroying my ship too, cause there is friendly fire in space, and got kicked, i joined the discord community to find people to play with and wow, theres' none. a lot of  them are like 30 years old no life angry gamer living ti grind, because there a ''grind'' system in the game of some sort, and if your new, no one help you or even  care, so in all, so buy and play with friends you know if this is the experience you want but for the public lobby part, it's filled with bad one's, so in all  its a fantastic game,  but with a terrible community

great game lots of fun. Could use some tweeking.  play it for a bit every night 
could use a little more info on what to do in game as a beginner player but once u figure it out its awesome. 
I'm using Protas controller mounts and the flying is superb 
looking forward to some more content and updates 
kudos to the dev team

I love everything about the game but nobody plays multiplayer I played this game yesterday and when I tried to press quick play it said there nobody playing even on the list so the next day the same thing happen where is everybody am I the only one who ever online

The gunplay is questionable, there isn’t a ton of variety in the missions after you complete a few, and the game has lots of AI use. (If you’re against that.)

However, the concept of the game is absolutely amazing and the execution is 7/10 I believe that if some polish is added to the current mechanics and more content is added (planets, missions, vehicles) this game could be a real gem.

Not a lot of players on so you should join the discord to have the best results with putting a team together… otherwise the game is amazing.. great gameplay loop. A lot of different mission types… Weapons… Ships.. and more.. I will be posting a review and gameplay on my YouTube: ZMeechyBaby

I enjoy flight sims and I enjoy first person shooters and this game combines the two in a very exciting and immersive way. Explore the surface of an enemy planet by jumping into a drop pod from your main battleship.  Fight giant robots and aliens with your blaster rifle to take out the laser turrets and find treasure.  Board your main ship and head to the hangar where you can fly your star fighter to fend off space pirates.  Fight your way into a giant enemy ship and destroy the power core to make it explode from the inside.  Jump back into hyperspace and rerun to your base to make repairs on your ship with all of your buddies on board.  Lots of different scenarios and upgrades to be achieved.  Thank you for making such a fun game!
Great game had a lot of potential but needs more players. Also having trouble finding a discord or some type of community for news and updates. Other than that it's my new favorite game

Interstellar Strike Team is an excellent co-op experience that truly shines when played with friends. The game offers a nice variety of mission types and solid opportunities to upgrade your gear. You can also purchase new fighters, which adds a great sense of progression and customization.

The co-op mode is where the game really stands out — it’s incredibly fun to coordinate attacks and tackle missions together. With the current amount of content, you can easily enjoy around 8 hours of gameplay before things start to look repeat.

For the next update, I’d love to see more weapons and equipment, additional mission types, and especially the ability to upgrade or customize your main battleship — there’s huge potential in that feature! Also, more enemy types would help keep the action fresh and challenging.

Overall, Interstellar Strike Team is a great co-op title with lots of promise and room to grow.

Almost impossible to play alone, but a lot of fun if you get a good team together. The community engagement is high and you may find yourself or your team in the news ticker, which is a nice touch. There is a lot of different tasks to do in any given mission, so no matter what kind of player you are, you should find a role you enjoy on a team and feel like you are contributing.
Maybe make it so you can fly the battle ship and fly in atmosphere please

I don't give out 5 star ratings lightly. This game is shockingly well put together. This is a display I'd developer talent that's sadly lacking in a lot of triple A studios these days.

Ship combat feels great. It brings back old space shooter vibes, but with intuitive controls and an updated feel.

Ground combat is serviceable. It can get intense, but it's not where the game shines.

It's in early access and actively developed and I'm excited to see what comes.

Personal wish list: 
- After burners for fighters! Go fast mode is cool and it would add a lot to dogfights. 

-Different guns and missile loadouts for fighters and your carrier. There might be resource limitations here, but it would be cool to be able to pick between rapid fire, spread shot, and cannon type weapons. 

-An on-foot mission type that takes place in a large interior space, like a large station or base.

reviews.review_title: I'd say it has great potential
reviews.review_description:
...if it was version 0.3, but the version number is 0.92 and the game is nowhere near 90% ready. At this stage it's a prototype at best.
I hope it doesn't mean anything and the score of four stars assumes the devs are aware of the ammount of work required.
But should it make it out of early access in this state, the score immediately goes down to 1.

I’m in love with the concept of this game but playing solo is a bit fun at times but also chaotic and almost rage inducing at others. The Extraction mission type for example was particularly frustrating as I had to wait for the timer to go down to find out I have to do math in order to advance the next section. I’m not really particularly good at math while I’m getting shot at by dozens of machines. Another issue I have is when you die you lose a good chunk of credits and you really only get a few credits every time you take out enemies. I do enjoy this game and I do highly recommend it just needs some touch ups here and there.

reviews.review_title: Might be a good game, but...
reviews.review_description: The walkthrough sucks. They show you a few things, then you're left on your own for the most part. If you click enough things, you'll find a mission. Also, the default controls suck, as the VR stick isn't responsive to you need to use the controller stick, and it doesn't map to how you'd expect, i.e. pull back to climb, push forward to dive. It's the opposite, as as far as I can tell, no way to easily change that. There is a setting, but it is completely not obvious what to set it to. Most games have a "reverse up/down" button to swap, this does not.

developer_response.body: I released an update that adds the option to invert the analog stick. Hope that helps.

reviews.review_description: Fantastic game. Would love to be able to fly the big ship instead of warping. But the pod is really nice. Also would love a resolution increase. But it’s the closest game I found to galaxy on fire so far.

reviews.review_title: I want to like it but..
reviews.review_description: Can you guys please make it single player friendly.... and more user friendly especially for single player

reviews.review_title: The multiplayer VR game I always wanted!
reviews.review_description:
Wow… to say I am impressed is an understatement. After 4+ hours of starting this game with a friend, I can confidently say this game is everything I ever wanted in a multiplayer VR game, gameplay wise.

The variety of mission types you can partake in keep things constantly fresh (with some feeling like Rogue Squadron space combat games & some others feeling like Helldivers drops onto planets). There are random events that take place that keep you on your toes. You can pilot your battleship’s turrets to take out enemies. You can fly in your own interceptor ship in space alongside others. There is something special about my friend controlling a turret from the battleship to watch over me, while I run and hop into my interceptor to take them out from space.

For me one HUGE feature that keeps you immersed through all this is there is little to no loading screens. Ride an elevator to the bridge of your battleship to warp somewhere new. Turn around, ride it back down, jump into your interceptor, fly through space, dock onto an enemy ship, board the ship and jump into a huge gunfight… it’s all seamless with no loading screens. This is a BIG REASON all of this works so well.

There is also a decent progression system in place already and a good amount of unlocks to grind for (weapons, interceptors, upgrades, camos, etc). The graphics are serviceable, game ran smooth, and honestly there wasn’t much jank for being an indie early access title. Sooo sooo impressed by this. This game has a ton of potential, and I think everyone should give it a try. I can’t speak to the solo experience, but if you have some friends or join a squad in game, this is honestly peak VR multiplayer. I never would have expected this from a $10 early access title.

BRAVO!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much. I am very glad that the intended immersion has worked good for you. Wasn't that easy to get it done without any loading screens, but this was an important aspect for the design to keep the immersion alive. Thank you very much and keep having fun in space. ;) PS: this review made my day.

reviews.review_description:
While this game is still in Early Access, it’s quickly become one of my top 5 games in VR, and I own quite a few big titles.

How’s this game compete with all the big names? Well, I’d say it’s because it’s got a little something for everyone. You can serve on the crew of your very own interstellar battleship, running around belowdecks making repairs or manning the helm operating the turrets. Additionally, if it’s more your speed, you can launch your own Interceptor fighter ship and have yourself a dogfight in space.

Even that would make for an interesting game, but there’s also a variety of planets to explore during ground missions. You get to take a Drop Pod to the planet’s surface with your choice of weapons and fight back on foot the way you want.

I began playing in late May and got my money’s worth out of it quite quickly. I was astonished to learn that there’s only one dev, extremely dedicated, who updates the game very frequently and is extremely receptive to feedback. This is absolutely a game worth giving a go.

I love space games, and this one reminds me of Mass Effect and From Other Suns, a bit of Star Trek Bridge Crew, and a bit of X-Wing. Even though it's in Early Access, the game is already fun and has a lot of potential, as the developer is constantly developing it. Plus, the price is ridiculously low, at $10 on the Meta Store for hours of great fun. I highly recommend it :)

I really hope the devs keep polishing the features already in place, because this game has so much potential for a bright future.

At the moment though, the terrain maps can feel a little empty, and playing solo without others sometimes turns into more of a grind.

Games like Pulsar: The Lost Colony and Deep Rock Galactic might be great sources of inspiration to help shape this into something truly special.

Too much fun! Lots of content already even with it being it early access still. Some small bugs with placement in seats when piloting and some mechanoids lagging out but for only 10$!! Worth it. Can't wait to see what else comes to it.

You can check out my actual review on Youtube under Gotcha VR.
If you enjoy Multiplayer, flying, or being on the ground shooting it up, then this is for you... It's still in early access but this Solo Dev is always updating, what's here is way better than most fully finished games.
The community is awesome right now, grab this whilst its at this fantastic price.

reviews.review_title: Awesome coop fun and great price
reviews.review_description: The review title says it all. Ive been looking for a great space fighter game and this has it all. FPS shooter and spaceship fighter, lots of upgradeable weapons and ships....just a ton of fun, and the price can't be beat.

Me and my team were forced to retreat from a space battle. We warped to what we were hoping would be a safe destination only to be ambushed mid warp by Pirates! After another fierce space battle we manged to limp our wrecked hull to a space station for repairs. We then played pool and listened to some decent tunes. For 10 dollars there is so much content. You can't go wrong with this game

I specifically look for multi-player games to play with my son. Been waiting for a game like this and its a lot better than I expected it to be when I bought it. Got in-space battles, on ground fighting, upgrades and level ups, starship dog fights, and decent customization. Both of us have been having fun in it. Obviously it got some kinks to work out and some things that can be improved but I am VERY much looking forward to the future of this game and anything this team creates in the future. You cannot go wrong for this price tag.

Me and my friends love to look for what we call “hidden gems” on the oculus and this one without a doubt was one of the best we have found so far. Even thought it is 10$ it amazing and very impressive for an early access game, I mean it feels so immersive and I really feel like I’m fighting in space. The variety of character creation and space ships is amazing and there’s a chill zone between the sectors which is always fun to stay in for a little if you’re tired of fighting. Over all this game is just outstanding compared to other early access games, just so much fun!!!!
Stuck on the blinking bear loading screen on my quest 2

developer_response.body: I'm sorry if you're having trouble starting the game. Normally, this only happens when the memory is full. However, the game has been optimized in this regard and such problems have not been reported since the memory optimization update. We always test on Quest 2. Are many other processes taking to much space in memory? Since I'm not aware of any blinking bears in the loading screen, my question would be whether this review ended up in the wrong app?

reviews.review_description: After trying it with some streamer friends we found this game has amazing bones and potential we just hope it see future evolution of mission type, side missions, story?, more ships weapons tech and upgrades and some fine turning of controls for newbie wouldn’t go amiss, amazing guys please keep building this!!!.

Well Done!!! 👏 👏 👏
P.s. massive drift from left hand controller with quest 2, many players are suffering from it.

You wanna be a bada$$ space mercenary then this is your game!! You can do ground or space combat and it has some weapons and ships to choose from already! For a early access game this is a solid 10/10 can't wait for more updates! Also under $10 this game has so much replay ability and to top it all off I Love the trench flying one it brings my childhood star wars dream to life! (Just gotta find that womprat size hole to shoot my torpedoes..)

Just played 2 hours with random guys.
We fought pirates, robots on planets and boarded big enemy battle ships. 
You can fly fighters and use turrets on the battleship, buy and upgrade weapons, do mission, land on planets etc 
This game is allready awesome 👍👍👍 lots of fun in coop

This is the 1st game I’ve given 5stars, this is the only game I’ve paid the full price for. If you want an amazing space pilot game, it’s here, want to crew a ship w/ friends, yep. Want to drop on a planet & clean up the leftovers? Yeah, it’s in here. I played about 17 minutes of the demo before I was hooked. I tried to speed past the rigmarole so I could optimize my time playing, & just wow, I’ve tried to find a “good” or even “decent” space shooter since I bought my headset, & I’m glad I landed on this gem of a game! I’ll gladly spend $10 to support this development if the beta version is this solid! Keep up the amazing work! The developers easily could’ve asked for $15-20, but $10? Why aren’t you playing this right now??
It sucks i can’t even fly my own interceptor without having to lose 100 dollars by crashing
Hoping this continues in that direction honestly.  Its alot of fun.  Hoping to see upgrades to ship systems and more in depth fighter ship customization

With alot of studios putting out early access that didn't work or over priced for hope that it will get better...this is amazing. 
ALOT of good things here with expected rough spots, landscapes and depth of missions. 
This is great for the price, fun to play with gun play as well as space fights, can't wait to see what's next.

Interstellar Strike Team is a hidden gem on the Meta Quest 2, offering a refreshing mix of exploration and team-based gameplay that’s been missing from many VR titles.

The game shines with its diverse mission types, giving players plenty of options whether you’re into tactical combat, objective-based challenges, taking over ships, salvage, or even simply dropping into a space station. It’s clear there’s a lot of potential here, and the devs seem to be building something special on top of what is already special  

The community has been very welcoming, which makes jumping in as a new player much easier. Controls feel intuitive, and the game encourages team coordination, which makes completing objectives incredibly satisfying.

Overall, I highly recommend Interstellar Strike Team if you’re looking for a VR experience that’s both engaging and rewarding. This is a game well worth your time and money, and it’s only getting better.w

I haven't  gotten a chance to actually play yet. I looked it up online to see if I could play this without Internet, and google said yes, google was wrong.
  Please try to make a way to play without Internet connection, I would love to be able to play this at home where I don't have internet.
  I don't even care if it needs to be a bigger game to be able to have this offline capability. 
  All I know is that I'm very saddened that I can't play this by myself at home, and am required to find somewhere with Wifi to play it. (Which just isn't gonna happen)

  I don't want to rate extremely low as I haven't played it. 
   If offline playability becomes available in the future, I would be more than happy to change my review.

developer_response.body: Hello, thanks for your review. I just released an update that adds the option to play solo offiline. Best regards.

reviews.review_title: Real gem in the Meta Store
reviews.review_description:
This game is a real gem in the Meta Store

The game is amazing, varied. Shooting with cannons on the main ship, flying ships and fighting in space, ground fighting on planets with opponents... in addition there are fights with space pirates that interrupt our warp travel...

Great ideas, great execution. The graphics could be better, but I understand the compromise compared to the entire content in the game.

I hope the game will develop well, there will be more missions, more fights, different opponents, ships, weapons and everything. A great game to gather a team (up to 6 players) and set off to conquer space
The Game Stand Alone Vr needed! Cant wait to see the future of this game. A muti-player gem...

I don't review games often, but since picking up IST I have been burning out my battery daily playing it. 

The gameplay is fantastic - space dogfights, ground assaults, base defence, trench runs, boarding enemy ships, all the stuff I love from games like the X-Wing series, Wing Commander, original Battlefront (showing my age). 

The design just feels like a love letter to the sci fi greats, particularly Battlestar Galactica. It looks and plays fantastic even on my old Quest 2.

The community is great, and unlike a lot of Quest games I play they generally seem to be adults.

For a single dev and an early access game this is a remarkable achievement and I can't wait to see where it goes from here.

Phoenix Gene is a fun and wonderful VR-based arcade style game with 8 different levels to complete and 7 individual powerups to earn (powerups are earned based on the number of “flame” points collected while playing.  The game can be played either sitting or standing, with various levels of difficulty to choose from. From the main menu you can choose to start the game where you left off, restart a previously completed level, or start an entirely new game.

I am testing this game during the early access stage and I found Phoenix Gene to have fewer bugs and glitches versus various similar games at full release. I believe that the obvious take away is that the full release will exceed expectations for games in this same genre. I really like this game, its challenges, and its story line. Failing to complete a certain task only makes me want to try again. Failing a task in a level restarts you back to the beginning of that specific task-related fail point. You are not required to restart at the beginning of the level itself (great time and frustration saver).

Pros
Excellent Graphics
Visually and performance-based
Beautiful, bold, and colorful opening graphic
Excellent audio
Individually adjustable sound controls
Music, master volume, etc
In-game sound effects are very appropriate for the arcade-style play environment
Light and optimized program code
Game loads/reloads quickly
Nicely constructed and intuitive game menu
Choice of continuing/restarting previously played levels or beginning a new and separate game
The green play button action is a little confusing at first.
  You have to ensure that the current level is selected or you will start from the “slow” beginning scene again at level 1
The game immediately restarts the same level should you get killed or not meet the requirements for level succession
 Interesting concept/feature allowing the player to “see/pass through” various objects and obtain level-related points or benefits
The tutorial is very basic and shows the player:
How to press the controller trigger button to fire at targets
How to maneuver the dragon you control by moving/extending your arms
Additional movement/control information is provided throughout the game as you earn powerups
Cons
Opening scene is confusing, too long, and confusing
You are floating along near a floor in a damaged room where it looks like beams/frames are reforming while a short background of what your purpose in the game is and who the main character named Jackie is
You cannot skip this scene and have to sit through around 15 seconds of waiting to get started
During this time, pressing the pause button does pause the game but you can only press the button again to unpause and continue floating
A “skip” option for this opening scene would be nice for players who have already seen this
Level 3 (in sitting/easy/relax mode game setting) player movement and orientation becomes confusing
The game-play direction changes so subtly that I was confused with what was happening.
I heard voices asking for help, who I needed to save, but did not know where the voices were coming from
ends up I had turned around 180 degrees and continuously failed to recover from my new movement direction to succeed the level
The in-game explanations regarding successfully adopting to the red or blue incoming “bullets” from the security drones was very brief
It took me some time to completely understand using the “A” or “X” buttons to change my dragon color to the appropriate incoming fire color and recognize the benefits
This becomes more manageable as you gain experience

developer_response.body: I think you posted your review to the wrong game?

reviews.review_description: Amazing game for early access,it like being on battlestar gallactica and star wars lol ,flying your ship through the trench makes u feel like your luke skywalker lol plenty to do and upgrade ,for price e eryone should own this game ,look forward to see wat else they do with it

Fly your mother ship and defend it with the turrets with your 6 person team in multiplayer. Fly your combat fighter. Run and gun with a rocket launcher. It's all good here bro.

Getting Star Wars Squadrons, Elite Dangerous, and Starship Trooper vibes. All hand motion controls for flight and ground combat work well. Fun solo or in multiplayer. 

Brand new to early release, I can't wait to see this game get popular. I been in VR over a decade now. Slow clap for this developer. o7
For The Mug

reviews.review_title: Love it, but I want more
reviews.review_description: I feel like I'm inside the most awesome ps2 game, but I hope they improve on it.  It can get repetitive. More of a story would be great moving forward.
The best game on vr
Wow, I really like this game. With some fine-tuning and more mission types, this game will be a legend in the VR world !

In only two hours, I learned how to fly a ship, fire the main guns and maintain a battle cruiser, board an enemy spaceport and blow it up like the Death Star, not to mention full-on star war-like battles, and met two of the nicest gentlemen who helped teach me. Had recommendations on how not to waste my credits and make it the best two-hour. And I can't wait to play again tomorrow!

If they manage to fix the Jank on the flight
 controls, this is easy 5 stars.

Wow thank You, i never got a rensponse from a Developer!
So my problem with the flight controls where: I am playing standing and when i try to tilt the controller forward, i always must twist my wrist very hard, much harder than in any other direction. Is it possible to fine-tune this? or maybe add an option to turn/tilt with thumbsticks? Would be awesome! My device is a quest 3, dont know if this is an issue with quest2. Thank You again for Interaction, modified to 5 stars, awesome game! :)

developer_response.body: Hello. I am sorry that you find the controls janky, but could you please explain more precisely, so I can try to improve the flight controls? Thank you very much in advance.

reviews.review_title: Love the versatility / game play options
reviews.review_description: This game is extremely good, especially for a single developer.  The potential is enormous!

The game does what it's already doing perfectly, everything is super well executed, it just needs MORE, more maps more mission types more stuff to grind for maybe even new enemy factions and ship upgrades

But since its early access I assume some stuff is coming so 5 stars
Do yourself a favor and just get this game. It’s so much fun for an early access game. It feels like a vr version of hell divers. Looking forward to see you in there!

reviews.review_title: This is a lot of fun
reviews.review_description: Had us yelling sci-fi movie quotes at each other uncontrollably!

!Excellent Game! This is a first person shooter where you can actually experience the “war” from the perspective of your own customizable character! There are a variety of mission types including ground missions against alien robots, flying aircraft in dramatic dog fighting missions, star-wars type flying missions, flying battle ships through obstacles to knock out the enemy, even the battleship has action with turrets and so much more!
One of the really cool things is that team week is essential, and players share the rewards! That means in a six player game, foot example, for players may go out to dog fight in planes, another may choose to use the turrets in the battleship while still another guards and repairs the ship, both the players onboard would defend the ship against enemy intrusion too! It’s crazy, intense and exhilarating fun

Star Wars fan? Star Trek? Star anything? Space combat- check. Planetary foot combat- check. Vehicles- check. Guns- check. What more could you ask for? This is THE scifi shooter in the Oculus VR world. Pilot interceptors, warp your command ship to missions, fight off enemy pirates. Stop enemies from boarding your ship, board the enemy ship and destroy it from within. Ground assaults. Buy ships, upgrade stats, earn medals and join the fight!!
That what this game is and it would be fun if we had mechs

This game reminds me a lot of another sci-fi game called Genesis Alpha One. The way you have the freedom to control so much of your ship and how you wish to handle tasks is perfect. I've only tried it out in solo so far, but maintaining the ship's functions is easily done, even for a lone player. Quick aside, thank you so much for making the turrets autonomous. Make's defense of the battleship a bit easier. 

If you enjoy a good sci-fi sandbox game that allows you control over aspects of your ship, this is your game.
I can't wait to see what they add next for this already amazing space game.

As an alpha release, I was a bit hesitant with this game, but holy shyte, it exceeded all of my expectations! It's a great game, that I can't wait to play more of.

I haven't found any issue, except in my opinion, the issue of needing at the bare minimum 2 people to play it in order to succeed at anything. And that's precisely what makes that "fault", an amazing experience. You really do need to play cooperatively to finish missions. And it's an amazing co-op game experience!

Looking forward to playing more of this with my group of friends!!!

Finally if you liked that game this one's like the next logical step. Man a spaceship with your buddies defend against boarding pirates with your guns, man the turret from the bridge, or go out and attack ships in your fighter. It also features away missions and enemy boarding missions. Keep showing this one love don't let it die like FoS!

reviews.review_title: Very fun and lots to do
reviews.review_description:
The graphics or the look of everything, is like Starship Troopers. Its very basic and not much detail. Like, the planets for instance, they are dark and gloomy with hills and big boulder type rocks. You have to find these small buildings with big turrets guns that are shooting up toward your main ship. You do your mission then hop back on your pod to get to the ship. There are no creatures, just robots. There is one vehicle you can purchase to get around the planet quicker but I just couldn't drive it. It bounced all over and staying in the front seat was difficult as well. You have the drivers side and then the passenger side you can control the big gun on top of the vehicle, EXCEPT, there really wasn't no way to control the aiming. I couldn't use it at all. But the vehicle was really cool looking. Kind of like a Batmobile. You purchase a vehicle for each planet. You don't just buy it and keep it.
    There are different types of missions. Go on planet or board enemy ships or fly a ship to shoot at enemies. Home base or mother ship is where you begin. You fly over to your main ship to go do missions. Then from there choose the type of mission you want. There are lots of missions. Maybe 15 different sections that have between 4-6 type of missions to do. So there is lots to do. However, they are pretty much the same missions over and over so it could get monotonous after awhile to some.
     You get to fly different types of ships to fight other ships in space. In other words, you upgrade your ships. It takes awhile to get used to how you fly. You have to keep your arm completely still and just move your wrist. It's small movements to turn, go up and down and to roll. At first I didn't understand why you needed to roll but after chasing other ships, it was needed and useful. If you get the hang of it, it's really fun to fly.
    The most creative part of this game is you also have to protect your main ship. You can work the turrets on this ship to destroy some enemy ships outside or just fly your small ship to do it instead. You can go to a planet but a pirate ship can attack the main ship while your gone. You may need to take the pod back to repair the ship by simply using a laser tool and turning the ship back on. Yet, your better off just finishing your mission first since it takes way too long to go back. While on planet, you can see an enemy ship flying around and you can shoot at it to destroy it. I dont know if they are pirate ships. It's too dark to tell.
     You can also fly your small ship to board an enemy ship and shoot your way through robots to destroy it. You can also fly your small ship to shoot at other enemy ships before they destroy your main ship.
    All in all, this game is very fun. The price is good for this game. Try it out even though the graphics aren't perfect and mechanics need work. It's a fun time with friends or just go alone. Only 4 people can go on the pod to go on planet. I've heard you can have lots of people play with you but as far as ALL of you going to a planet or enemy ship, I don't know. I do hope this game gets upgrades so it doesn't get boring doing the same type of missions. I know they are still working on this game and they're doing a good job.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. I'm sorry you had problems with the vehicle controls. The turret automatically aims in the direction you are looking, but you have to grip the controls with both hands. The same applies to the driver. Sometimes the terrain is too steep to accelerate and it's better to maneuver backwards and gain momentum again. About the pod: players can share a seat, although this is not ideal. I hope that helps.

reviews.review_description: Interstellar Strike Team combines FPS shooting, dogfighting, and base upkeep. It has a seamless communication system and even offers quick comm macros available from your wrist. This game requires consistent teamwork to ease the completion of missions. Most missions can be soloed, but takes much longer to complete. I really appreciate that while dogfighting and doing some other missions, your base ship "Destroyer" must be protected.  Purchase this game. You'll be glad you did.

reviews.review_title: A good game with great potential!
reviews.review_description: Myself and three others picked this game up looking to try something different, and we all came away from it incredibly impressed. The core gameplay loop is solid, the fighter controls are phenomenally smooth and the Destroyer management (defense, repair, etc) feels great. There are some issues, which are to be expected with any kind of EA release, such as randomly losing the ability to grip the yoke for a few seconds when first loading into space from the freighter. Having to move through the ship repairing damage and fending off boarders is freakin' sweet, though the on-foot gameplay is definitely its weakest element currently in terms of the weapon handling and general lack of interaction (at least on the weapons we tried). You pick up a floating mag to reload, which is fine, but it would be great to have additional interactions such as being able to prime the weapon with your off hand (i.e. using trigger to rack the slide) and when stabilizing the rifle with your offhand it just slides around oddly instead of gripping it properly. It's similar to Guardians Frontline in terms of having infinite ammunition, but needs a lot of love before it plays as smooth. The muzzle flash is a bit excessive as well, making it difficult to accurately stay on target, but that may be by design. My biggest concern for the moment is the fact that the smooth turn is incredibly slow, and I would love to see them add the ability to increase its speed. All that said, the game is a blast, especially if you have a few others to hop into it with. I look forward to seeing how it evolves in the future!
Super fun to play with friends! But I would like to see some helmets in the game plus different biomes and actual aliens to fight. But over all it’s a great game with plenty to do!
I am honoured to have found this game while looking for something fun to play, it’s easily become one of my most played games and I’m so excited to see what this game becomes in the future.

Purchased in the sale after reading the
 reviews for under £6 and haven't played anything else since. Tried a couple of games in solo to work out the mechanics then jumped into multiplayer. Very similar to Pulsar Lost Colony on PC  it's a fun multiplayer space combat game  where you can pilot ships or try your hand at ground missions, someone  described it as Elite Dangerous Lite I can't recommend it enough great game

reviews.review_title: Best Sci Fi game out there!
reviews.review_description:
This game is so fun when you understand what to do. Like going to different planets, blowing up spaceships, and doing fun missions. This game already looks fun when it is an early development. The only game I can say that is similar to this is Helldivers 2. 

Now, I would say there are some minor fixes. Like when I am on a planet, like to destroy some robot bases. I don’t know where to go or don’t have a map. Also the robots enemies can shoot me with four shots and I am dead. It is kinda a bit too hard for one enemy.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review. During ground missions, your tablet (left shoulder) displays a map on which the targets, your teammates and yourself are marked. ;)

reviews.review_description: This has been a hell of an experience, it's got missions that scratch every itch, star wars space battles, helldivers ground objectives and terminator 2 base defense. Please buy this game and play public, it's entirely co-op and everyone has been great that I've played with, even as a newbie they'll guide you through it. Looking forward to see what else comes in the future for this game, enemy boarding parties would be awesome!

reviews.review_title: Early days but looks really good so far!
reviews.review_description:
I have only spent about an hour on this game so far, but enjoyed the gun turret combat from the main bridge, and drop ship downriver a planet for some FPS action… graphics are great too and I will get around to some multiplayer soon to improve my mission performance!
Great job, keep it up 👍

reviews.review_title: Feels like I'm in Battlestar Galactica.
reviews.review_description: There's nothing like this game currently on the market for VR. There's so much cool stuff here from the gear, weapons, ships, missions, space battles, land battles, etc. I did a couple of solo missions and then went and tried multiplayer. It was so cool having a job on the battleship and then having to hop into an interceptor for additional support. Having a crew was amazing and I'm not normally a multiplayer fan. I'm excited to see where this game goes from here. I would recommend this game to any sci-fi fan!
So basically worthless unless other people are online to play.

developer_response.body: Hello I am sorry that you couldn't find a team. The difficulty 1 and 2 missions are doable as solo also. To meet up with teams you can check our Discord. There are really active players. Link can be found in the main menu. Update: player count increased drastically, so chances are much better now to find an ongoing crew, but of couse not 100%. Please keep in mind, this is still early access.

reviews.review_description: Ambitious but overwhelming and boring.

developer_response.body: Hi, I'm sorry that you don't like the game. More information would be helpful to improve the game. What exactly did you find boring or what types of missions? What do you think could be done to make it less overwhelming? Thank you very much.

reviews.review_description:
A mix of fps and space dog fighting. 
It's a really fun team based game too...
We had great fun launching from our ship to intercept incoming fighters... And fighting our way on planets to destroy alien bases.. The weapon system is really well done.. No issues with holsters no issues reloading a very pleasant experience.. 
Look forward to seeing how this game evolves because, as it stands in early access, it's really well done...
Once you get the hang of the controls and game modes, it's so much fun... 
Well worth a look....

This game is really cool. It reminds me of a game I am waiting to release for PC, and Xbox called jumpship. The art style of this game, the ships specifically, reminds me of Star Citizen.

An all around fun game and I love the missions. I am hooked.

I will say, though. The game should be a little harder, specifically when you have more than one person. Also, I suggest advertising more because the game could use a lot more players.

The game is in early access, so I will update my review as the game progress, but for now, it's a solid 4 out of 5.

reviews.review_title: The Space Sim we all needed
reviews.review_description:
First of all, it's in early access but it's so much to do, sure it has minor bugs that can be over looked the overall game is a blast to play 

4 Player Multiplayer, (6 if your playing a private session) several different mission types you can fly, fire rockets from your main ship at targets on the planet, or fly into space for some dog fighting while your friends are shooting the torrents on the main ship or  joining you in the dog fight 

That's not even the half, you can go on planets and do missions your can even call for a vehicle 

It's so much going on in this game it's overwhelming 

It's an XP system you earn money from your missions and kills your level up 

I highly recommend getting this and if you have fiends that have a quest urge then to get this hidden gem

To the devs have a setting to turn off the blinders when you run and add more walking inside of the space station add some npc's walking around to give it some life and please start a discord server, other than that it's a great game in this early access state

developer_response.body: Hello, thank you very much for your very positive feedback. We have set up a Discord and are currently preparing an update that will include a notice about the feedback channel: https://discord.gg/JmCEHytJ

reviews.review_description: Like another reviewer, I look for multiplayer and coop games to play with my son. This game is what VR was made for. It is absolutely amazing.
hellla fun and its only in alpha

reviews.review_title: New and a fresh take on Coop VR
reviews.review_description:
I was skeptical when I saw the trailer, but you can do it all. My friends and I bought the game and a few hours later, we had to take a break. Sleeping and eating is a must, so yeah.  Overall, the beginnings of something great!!!

That said, a few bugs were persistent. In my case, when I try to dock a fighter the game gets stuck, but only for me. I'm on Quest 3.

Next, we were in a private game, so we could have 5 players. At some point 2 of the players were not visible to the rest of us. They could play, but not visible.  A fresh host session fixed it .

The resolution settings are too low and the game could use gamma/ brightness controls. It is too dark in many areas. 

Need more fine control over joystick movement and speed, especially for turrets and vehicles. Low, med, and high are not good enough.  Even low is too fast for turrets firing long distance and zoomed.

Avatar runs look weird, very mechanical, not organic.
None of the discord links seem to work, so reporting bugs here.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for your review and for reporting the bugs. This helps us a lot! The Discord link should be fixed now:

https://discord.gg/aGmuD9bhPj

I hope you can still enjoy the game. :)

reviews.review_description: Incredible game especially considering it’s early access. There may not be a lot of active players but getting on with the boys is an amazing experience. Hope the devs keep at it so the game gets the attention it deserves. If you haven’t tried it yet I highly recommend. Affordable and tons to experience. Haven’t had any bugs as of yet.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your feedback. We will do our best.
